name: Chocolate Ice Cream
author: Ice Cream Science
comments: Source: https://www.icecreamscience.com/blog/chocolate-ice-cream-recipe, visited 2026-07-07. The preparation instructions ask for the dairy, sugar, and egg yolk mix to be reduced by 15%, which works out to 150g of water evaporated. The recipe states '1 tsp vanilla extract', which is ~5g. The recipe asks for '3g whole coffee beans' to be heated with the mix and then strained out. This has a negligible effect on the mix composition; I suspect that it's equivalent to a tiny amount of instant coffee, so I have modeled it as 1g of instant coffee. The recipe does not specify a fat content for the cocoa powder; 17% fat is a common mid-range value.

name: Sweet Cream Ice Cream
author: Ice Cream Science
comments: Source: https://www.icecreamscience.com/blog/homemade-sweet-cream-ice-cream-recipe, visited 2026-07-10. The recipe uses 'semi-skimmed milk', which I assume is ~2% partly skimmed milk. The preparation instructions ask for the mix to be concentrated by "about 32%", which works out to 352g of water evaporated, for a final yield of ~750g.

name: Pistachio Ice Cream
author: Ice Cream Science
comments: Source: https://www.icecreamscience.com/blog/roasted-pistachio-ice-cream-recipe, visited 2026-07-10. The preparation instructions ask for 15% weight reduction of the pre-pistachio mixture, which works out to 150g of water evaporated.

name: Vanilla Bean Ice Cream
author: Ice Cream Science
comments: Source: https://www.icecreamscience.com/blog/vanilla-bean-ice-cream-recipe, visited 2026-07-17. The preparation instructions ask for a post-evaporation weight of 871g, which works out to ~180g of water evaporated. The recipe states '2 tsp vanilla extract', which is ~10g, and one vanilla bean pod.

name: Ninja Creami NC301 Easy No-Cook Tres Leches (US)
author: Ice Cream Science
comments: Source: https://www.icecreamscience.com/blog/ninja-creami-nc30-easy-no-cook-tres-leches-recipe, visited 2026-07-06. Sci-Cream's composition of the ingredients used in the recipe do not agree with Ice Cream Science's composition - milk is 3.23% vs 3.6% fat, cream is 33.4% vs 32.4% fat, and most importantly, sweetened condensed milk is 46% vs ~58% sucrose. As a result, the mix composition differs significantly from Ice Cream Science's composition, with total solids 41.1% vs 46.34%, MSNF 10.9% vs 12.16%, fat 15.5% vs 16.02%, and sucrose 14.5% vs 18.15%. This is the US version of the recipe; see "Ninja Creami NC301 Easy No-Cook Tres Leches (UK)" for the UK version. This is a no-cook recipe intended to be made using the Ninja Creami NC301 ice cream maker; I am not sure how it would perform in a standard ice cream maker.

name: Ninja Creami NC301 Easy No-Cook Tres Leches (UK)
author: Ice Cream Science
comments: Source: https://www.icecreamscience.com/blog/ninja-creami-nc30-easy-no-cook-tres-leches-recipe, visited 2026-07-06. Sci-Cream's resulting mix composition matches Ice Cream Science's stated composition to within ~5%. This is the UK version of the recipe; see "Ninja Creami NC301 Easy No-Cook Tres Leches (US)" for the US version. This is a no-cook recipe intended to be made using the Ninja Creami NC301 ice cream maker; I am not sure how it would perform in a standard ice cream maker.
